context:
<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Marcus Johnson  
- **Age:** 32  
- **Gender:** male  
- **Experience level:** **Intermediate** — ~4 years total lifting, ~2.5 years focused powerlifting; competed in USAPL local meet (Nov 2024), went 8/9, placed 3rd in **220 lb class**.

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):** Peak for upcoming powerlifting meet (currently **12 weeks out as of 2026-02-27**)  
  - Increase total **1350 lb → 1450 lb**
  - **Squat:** 455 → 485  
  - **Bench:** 315 → 340  
  - **Deadlift:** 580 → 625
- **Secondary goals:**
  - Fix bench sticking point **~2–3 inches off chest**
  - Improve **squat depth consistency** (meet-standard depth, repeatable)

---

## TRAINING CONTEXT

### Schedule & Availability
- **Training frequency:** 4x/week
- **Training days:** **Mon / Tue / Thu / Fri**
- **Time window:** **5:30 AM** sessions
- **Session duration:** not specified

### Equipment & Environment
- **Location:** Commercial gym — **Iron Haven Powerlifting Gym (Tempe, AZ)**
- **Equipment available (specific):**
  - Competition-spec power racks, monolifts
  - Specialty bars: **SSB, Swiss bar, Texas deadlift bar**
  - **Bands, chains**
  - Full dumbbell rack to **150 lb**
  - **GHD, reverse hyper**
  - Standard barbells/plates

### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Chronic right shoulder impingement — aggravated by overhead work or wide-grip bench; cannot do strict overhead press; uses **landmine variations**; manages with daily band work; sees sports PT monthly (**since 2026-02-27**). Monitor symptoms and bench grip selection closely during peak.

### Preferences
- Prefers **percentage-based programming** and **DUP**
- Familiar with **RPE** and able to autoregulate
- Powerlifting-specific environment; goal-driven meet prep focus
- Exercise selection accommodations: avoid strict overhead pressing; avoid wide-grip bench when shoulder is irritated

---

## METRICS
*(Ordered for powerlifting priority. All “current” values recorded **as of 2026-02-27** unless otherwise noted.)*

### Strength (Powerlifting)
- **Total:** **1350 lb** (as of 2026-02-27)
- **Squat:** **455 lb** (current best as of 2026-02-27)
- **Bench Press:** **315 lb** (current best as of 2026-02-27)
- **Deadlift:** **580 lb** (current best as of 2026-02-27)

**Technical/Performance Notes**
- Bench: sticking point **2–3 inches off chest** (as of 2026-02-27)
- Squat: depth consistency needs improvement (as of 2026-02-27)

### Body Composition
- **Weight:** not provided (note: previously competed in **220 lb class**, Nov 2024)

---

## LOG
## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (meet prep baseline)
- Set primary goal: increase powerlifting total **1350 → 1450** within **~12 weeks** (meet date not specified).
- Captured lift targets: SQ **455→485**, BP **315→340**, DL **580→625**.
- Noted key limiters: bench sticking point **2–3" off chest**; squat **depth consistency**.
- Training schedule confirmed: **4x/week (Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri), 5:30 AM**, at Iron Haven Powerlifting Gym (Tempe, AZ).
- Constraint added: **[ACTIVE] chronic right shoulder impingement**; overhead pressing avoided; landmine variations used; monthly sports PT; wide-grip bench aggravates symptoms.

If you share your current bodyweight, meet date/federation, and most recent *rep-based* top sets (e.g., 1@8, 3@8) for S/B/D, I can tighten the baseline metrics and make the “12 weeks out” timeline fully consistent.</Profile>

reference.response:
## Header
**Program name:** 12-Week USAPL Meet Peak — DUP 4-Day (Depth + Off-Chest Bench Focus)  
**Program Owner:** GymText  
**User:** Marcus Johnson  
**Goal:** Peak for powerlifting meet in ~12 weeks; raise total 1350 → 1450 (SQ 455→485 / BP 315→340 / DL 580→625)  
**Duration:** 12 weeks (meet prep)

---

## Program Philosophy
Marcus is an intermediate powerlifter with meet experience, a clear 12-week timeline, and a preference for percentage-based DUP—so this plan uses competition lifts 2–3x/week exposure across the week while undulating stress (volume/technique early week, heavier specificity later). Squat depth consistency is addressed by frequent competition-standard squatting plus pauses/tempos and explicit depth constraints; bench’s 2–3" off-chest weakness is targeted via paused bench + Spoto/1-count variations and triceps/pec accessories without aggravating shoulder impingement. Because overhead work and wide-grip bench can flare the right shoulder, pressing volume is kept specific and joint-friendly (moderate grip, Swiss bar as needed, no strict OHP; landmine only for “health” work). Progression is percentage-based with RPE guardrails, planned deloads (Weeks 4 and 8), and a realization/taper to practice openers and reduce fatigue while preserving speed and confidence.

---

# Phase 1: Accumulation / Technical Base (Weeks 1–4)
**Goals:** build work capacity, lock in repeatable meet depth, add bench volume without shoulder flare, and accumulate quality reps.  
**Deload:** Week 4.

## Weekly Pattern

### **Mon — Squat Volume + Bench Technique**
- **Focus:** squat volume + depth standardization; bench technique and off-chest strength
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets

1) **Competition Back Squat (depth judged)**: 5 × 5 @ 72–77% (RPE 6–7)  
   *Primary volume driver; every rep to meet depth with identical setup/bracing.*

2) **Pause Squat (1–2 sec in the hole)**: 3 × 3 @ 65–72% (RPE 6–7)  
   *Reinforces depth control and tightness; removes bounce/“high” reps.*

3) **Competition Bench Press (paused)**: 5 × 4 @ 70–76% (RPE 6–7)  
   *High-quality paused reps to strengthen start + consistent commands.*

4) **Chest-Supported Row**: 4 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
5) **Triceps (cable pressdown or DB rolling extension)**: 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 7  
6) **Shoulder prehab (band ER + scap retraction)**: 2–3 short rounds (pain-free)  
   *Supports impingement management without provoking it.*

---

### **Tue — Deadlift Volume + Bench Off-Chest**
- **Focus:** deadlift volume; bench weakness 2–3" off chest
- **Volume:** ~15–20 working sets

1) **Competition Deadlift (stiff/power bar preferred)**: 4 × 4 @ 70–76% (RPE 6–7)  
   *Builds base pulling strength; keep bar path tight and consistent setup.*

2) **Paused Deadlift (1" off floor, 1 sec)**: 3 × 3 @ 60–70% (RPE 6–7)  
   *Improves position off the floor and lat tension.*

3) **Spoto Press (pause 1–2" off chest)**: 4 × 5 @ 67–74% (RPE 6–7)  
   *Directly targets 2–3" off-chest control and force production.*

4) **Lat pulldown / weighted pull-up**: 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7  
5) **Reverse Hyper**: 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 7  
6) **Optional: curls**: 2 × 10–15

---

### **Thu — Squat Technique + Bench Volume (Shoulder-Friendly)**
- **Focus:** squat patterning + speed; bench hypertrophy with joint-friendly options
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets

1) **Tempo Squat (3 sec down, controlled depth)**: 4 × 4 @ 60–70% (RPE 6)  
   *Grooves depth and positions; reduces “dive-bombing” that causes high reps.*

2) **SSB Squat (moderate stance)**: 3 × 6 @ RPE 7  
   *Upper back/bracing strength and quad stimulus without needing maximal loads.*

3) **Close-Grip Bench Press (moderate close; pain-free)**: 5 × 5 @ 70–77% (RPE 6–7)  
   *More triceps and better shoulder tolerance than wide grip; still specific.*

4) **DB incline (neutral grip) OR machine press**: 2–3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
   *Only if shoulder feels good; skip if impingement is irritated.*

5) **1-arm cable row**: 3 × 10–12 @ RPE 7  
6) **Face pull + band pull-apart superset**: 2–3 × 15–25  
   *Scapular mechanics support benching tolerance.*

---

### **Fri — Bench Heavy + Deadlift Technique**
- **Focus:** heavier bench exposure; deadlift speed/positioning
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) **Competition Bench Press (paused)**:  
- Top single: 1 × 1 @ 82–88% (RPE 7–8)  
- Back-offs: 4 × 3 @ 75–80% (RPE 7)

   *Weekly “practice heavy” without grinding; build confidence and skill.*

2) **2-Count Paused Bench (long pause)**: 3 × 3 @ 70–75% (RPE 7)  
   *Builds strength off the chest and command discipline.*

3) **Deadlift (speed)**: 6 × 2 @ 60–67% (RPE 6), 60–90s rest  
   *Keeps pulling frequency without accumulating fatigue.*

4) **GHD hip extension or hamstring curl**: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
5) **Farmer carry or suitcase carry**: 3 × 30–45 sec  
   *Bracing and trunk stiffness for squat/deadlift stability.*

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
- **Percent-based weekly increases:** add **+2.5%** to primary lifts week-to-week *if* all top sets stay within target RPE.
- **RPE guardrails:** if a prescribed set exceeds the top of the range (e.g., RPE 8 when it should be 6–7), reduce load **2.5–5% that day** and complete work with perfect execution.
- **Bench shoulder rule:** any front-shoulder pinch or symptom escalation → switch the day’s bench variation to **Swiss bar bench** (neutral grip) at same sets/reps, and keep RPE ≤7.
- **Deload (Week 4):** reduce **volume ~45–55%** (halve accessory sets; reduce main lift sets), keep intensity ~80–85% of Week 3 loads (i.e., still move moderately heavy but fewer reps).

---

# Phase 2: Intensification / Strength (Weeks 5–8)
**Goals:** increase specificity and intensity, convert volume to strength, keep depth/paused discipline, and raise heavy single proficiency.  
**Deload:** Week 8.

## Weekly Pattern

### **Mon — Squat Heavy Volume + Bench Technique**
- **Focus:** heavier squat sets; maintain paused bench volume
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) **Competition Back Squat**: 5 × 3 @ 80–85% (RPE 7–8)  
2) **Pause Squat**: 3 × 2 @ 72–78% (RPE 7–8)  
   *Depth + tightness under heavier loading.*

3) **Competition Bench (paused)**: 4 × 4 @ 75–80% (RPE 7)  
4) **Row (machine or chest-supported)**: 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8  
5) **Triceps (pressdown / JM press if elbow-friendly)**: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8

---

### **Tue — Deadlift Strength + Off-Chest Bench**
- **Focus:** heavier deadlift; bench off-chest intensification
- **Volume:** ~13–17 working sets

1) **Competition Deadlift**: 4 × 3 @ 80–85% (RPE 7–8)  
2) **Deadlift variation (choose ONE):**  
- **Paused deadlift 1" off floor**: 3 × 2 @ 72–78% (RPE 7–8) **OR**  
- **2" deficit deadlift**: 3 × 2 @ 70–76% (RPE 7)  
   *Pick based on what best addresses your weakest pull position; keep it crisp.*

3) **Spoto Press**: 5 × 3 @ 75–82% (RPE 7–8)  
4) **Lat work (pulldown/pull-up)**: 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 8  
5) **Reverse Hyper**: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7–8

---

### **Thu — Squat Secondary + Bench Volume (Joint-Friendly)**
- **Focus:** squat pattern at moderate load; bench volume without irritation
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) **Competition Squat (speed/technique)**: 6 × 2 @ 70–75% (RPE 6–7)  
   *Fast, identical reps to lock in depth + ascent mechanics.*

2) **SSB Squat OR high-bar squat**: 3 × 5 @ RPE 7–8  
3) **Close-Grip Bench (paused)**: 5 × 4 @ 75–80% (RPE 7–8)  
4) **Face pulls + band ER**: 3 × 15–25  
5) **Row variation**: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7

---

### **Fri — Bench Heavy + Deadlift Technique**
- **Focus:** heavy paused bench; low-fatigue pulling practice
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets

1) **Competition Bench Press (paused)**:  
- Top single: 1 × 1 @ 88–92% (RPE 8)  
- Back-offs: 4 × 2 @ 82–86% (RPE 7–8)

2) **Long-Pause Bench (2-count)**: 3 × 2 @ 75–80% (RPE 7–8)  
3) **Deadlift (speed)**: 5 × 2 @ 65–70% (RPE 6–7)  
4) **Hamstring curl or GHD**: 2–3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- **Main lifts:** move from triples toward doubles while increasing intensity **~2–3% per week** if RPE targets are met.
- **Singles:** should look like meet reps—no grinders. If the single hits RPE 9 early, cap the day and reduce back-offs by 2.5–5%.
- **Accessory volume:** keep enough to support shoulders/back/triceps, but trim anything that creates soreness that disrupts main lift performance.
- **Deload (Week 8):** reduce volume **50–60%**, keep one “taste” single at ~85% (RPE 6–7) for each comp lift if you feel good; otherwise skip singles.

---

# Phase 3: Realization / Peak + Taper (Weeks 9–12)
**Goals:** express strength, practice attempts and commands, minimize fatigue, arrive fresh and confident.  
**Week 12:** taper into meet (meet assumed end of Week 12).

## Weekly Pattern

### **Mon — Squat + Bench (Opener Practice Progression)**
- **Focus:** squat specificity; bench practice at moderate volume
- **Volume:** ~8–12 working sets

**Weeks 9–10**
1) **Competition Squat**: work to 1 × 1 @ 90–94% (RPE 8–8.5), then 3 × 2 @ 80–85%  
2) **Competition Bench (paused)**: 4 × 3 @ 80–85% (RPE 7–8)

**Week 11**
1) **Competition Squat**: 1 × 1 @ ~92–96% (RPE 8–9), then 2 × 2 @ ~80%  
2) **Competition Bench**: 3 × 2 @ ~82–86%

**Week 12 (early week)**
- **Squat:** 3 × 1 @ ~70–75% (RPE 6), all commands/depth  
- **Bench:** 3 × 1 @ ~70–75% (RPE 6)

---

### **Tue — Deadlift (Heavy Single) + Bench Off-Chest (Minimal)**
- **Focus:** heavy deadlift single; small dose off-chest bench
- **Volume:** ~7–11 working sets

**Weeks 9–10**
1) **Competition Deadlift**: 1 × 1 @ 90–94% (RPE 8–8.5), then 3 × 2 @ 80–85%  
2) **Spoto Press (light/moderate)**: 3 × 3 @ 75–80% (RPE 7)

**Week 11**
1) **Competition Deadlift**: 1 × 1 @ ~92–96% (RPE 8–9), then 2 × 2 @ ~80%  
2) **Bench technique (paused)**: 2 × 2 @ ~80% (easy)

**Week 12 (early week)**
- **Deadlift:** 2–3 × 1 @ ~70–75% (RPE 6), speed focus

---

### **Thu — Bench Heavy (Last Heavy Bench) + Squat Technique**
- **Focus:** final bench heavy exposure; keep squat sharp
- **Volume:** ~7–10 working sets

**Weeks 9–10**
1) **Competition Bench (paused)**: 1 × 1 @ 90–94% (RPE 8–8.5), then 3 × 2 @ 82–86%  
2) **Competition Squat (technique)**: 4 × 2 @ 72–78% (RPE 6–7)

**Week 11 (last heavy bench)**
1) **Competition Bench**: 1 × 1 @ ~92–96% (RPE 8–9), then 2 × 2 @ ~82%  
2) **Squat (very light)**: 3 × 2 @ ~70%

**Week 12**
- If meet is Sat/Sun: **OFF** or very light mobility/bands only.

---

### **Fri — Primer / Warm-Up Session (Very Low Fatigue)**
- **Focus:** blood flow, confidence, commands; no soreness
- **Volume:** ~4–6 working sets (or off)

**Weeks 9–11**
- **Bench (paused)**: 3 × 1 @ 75–80% (RPE 6)  
- **Row (light)**: 2 × 8–10

**Week 12**
- Typically **OFF** (or 15–20 min very easy movement + band routine).

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- **Singles progression:** Week 9 → 10 → 11 increases toward likely attempts while staying submaximal (no missed reps).
- **Volume drops each week:** accessories nearly eliminated by Week 11 to reduce fatigue.
- **Taper rules (Week 12):** keep intensity “snappy” but low volume; nothing that causes soreness; prioritize sleep and consistent warm-ups.

---

## Phase Cycling
This is a meet-specific 12-week peak: **Accumulation (1–4) → Intensification (5–8) → Realization/Taper (9–12)**. After the meet, transition into **2–4 weeks of post-meet recovery + hypertrophy/base rebuilding** (lower intensity, moderate volume, shoulder-friendly exercise variety), then restart a new cycle with updated maxes and any new constraints. Phase transitions are time-based here, but also readiness-based: if shoulder symptoms spike or performance stalls for >2 weeks, reduce bench stress (swap to Swiss bar for volume, keep comp bench for singles only) and extend the current phase’s loading another week before progressing.

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created (12-week DUP meet prep). Shoulder impingement accommodations added (no strict OHP; moderate grip; Swiss bar as pain-free fallback). Bench off-chest weakness targeted with paused bench + Spoto/long-pause work. Squat depth consistency targeted with frequent comp squats plus tempo/pause squats.

--- 

If you share (1) your exact meet date, (2) whether it’s USAPL again, (3) your most recent heavy singles like **1@8** (or **3@8**) for S/B/D, I can lock in Week 9–11 single percentages to match your current e1RMs and tighten opener/second/third attempt ranges.
